title: Check commoner-probe PyPI name availability and reservation
description: Package is now named commoner-probe (was sansad-crawler). Re-check PyPI availability/squatting for commoner-probe and document reservation strategy. The earlier sansad-crawler check is obsolete.
design: Prefer a read-only PyPI query first. Treat any placeholder publication as an irreversible release action, not a routine verification command.
acceptance_criteria: commoner-probe availability on PyPI confirmed and documented; reservation approach decided; no upload without owner approval.
notes:
Corresponds to blocker 3 in .ai/plans/pypi-release.md.
A prior availability check was completed in sansad-crawler-gis.3 on 2026-05-22. This new gate requires a fresh read-only check immediately before release because name ownership can change over time.
close_reason: Verified: commoner-probe returns HTTP 404 on PyPI (available). commoner (exists, unrelated utilities package by different author) poses no conflict. pyproject.toml already uses name=commoner-probe. No reservation action needed until upload.

title: Verify AGPL-3.0 license in distribution artifacts
description: License is now AGPL-3.0-or-later (PR 6 replaced MIT). Verify LICENSE file, pyproject license field, and PyPI classifier all say AGPL-3.0-or-later, and LICENSE ships in sdist+wheel.
design: Verify the existing MIT state during artifact inspection; avoid changing licensing absent a new explicit maintainer decision.
acceptance_criteria: LICENSE is AGPL-3.0 text; pyproject license=AGPL-3.0-or-later; classifier is AGPLv3+; LICENSE present in built artifacts; no MIT references remain.
notes: Corresponds to blocker 1 in .ai/plans/pypi-release.md. Implementation was previously completed in sansad-crawler-gis.1; retained here only as a release-artifact verification gate.
close_reason: Verified: LICENSE=AGPL-3.0 text, pyproject license=AGPL-3.0-or-later + AGPLv3+ classifier, LICENSE ships in both sdist and wheel (commoner_probe-0.3.0), zero MIT references remaining.

title: Verify 'sansad-crawler' name is unclaimed on PyPI
description: Before building/uploading anything, confirm the PyPI distribution name 'sansad-crawler' (and the normalised form 'sansad_crawler') is not already taken. If taken, the release plan must rename the package before any other blocker is worth resolving.
acceptance_criteria:
- Run: pip index versions sansad-crawler   (should report 'No matching distribution found' or similar 'not on PyPI').
- Also visit https://pypi.org/project/sansad-crawler/ and confirm 404.
- Record the verification (date + command output) in this issue's notes before closing.
- If the name IS taken: do not close this issue; instead open a follow-up decision bead about renaming, and block the epic on it.
notes: Source: .ai/plans/pypi-release.md section 'Blockers' #3. Plan also recommends reserving the name early via a 0.0.1 placeholder upload once blockers #1 and #2 are resolved, but that reservation upload is out of scope for this verification issue.
close_reason: Verified 2026-05-22: pip index versions sansad-crawler returns no matching distribution. Name is available on PyPI.

title: Add [project.urls] block to pyproject.toml
description: Without project URLs, the PyPI project page shows no homepage, source, issue tracker, docs, or changelog links. This makes the package look abandoned and hurts discoverability.
acceptance_criteria:
- pyproject.toml contains a [project.urls] table with at minimum:
    Homepage      = https://github.com/CommonerLLP/sansad-crawler
    Source        = https://github.com/CommonerLLP/sansad-crawler
    Issues        = https://github.com/CommonerLLP/sansad-crawler/issues
    Documentation = https://github.com/CommonerLLP/sansad-crawler/blob/master/README.md
    Changelog     = https://github.com/CommonerLLP/sansad-crawler/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md
- Verify the URL targets actually exist (org name 'CommonerLLP' and default branch 'master' are correct for this repo).
- python -m build succeeds and twine check dist/* passes.
- The rendered URLs appear in the wheel's METADATA (Project-URL: lines).
notes: Source: .ai/plans/pypi-release.md section 'Blockers' #2.
close_reason: [project.urls] block added to pyproject.toml with Homepage, Source, Issues, Documentation, Changelog URLs.

title: Add MIT LICENSE file
description: pyproject.toml declares license = PolyForm-Noncommercial-1.0.0 but the repo has no LICENSE file. Without one, GitHub does not show the license badge, pip 24+ SPDX scanners flag it as missing, and downstream tooling (Dependabot, OSS scorecards) treats the package as unlicensed.
acceptance_criteria:
- A file named LICENSE exists at the repo root.
- Contents are the canonical PolyForm Noncommercial 1.0.0 text from https://polyformproject.org/licenses/noncommercial/1.0.0/ (verbatim, no edits).
- File is included in the sdist (verify via: python -m build --sdist && tar tzf dist/sansad_crawler-*.tar.gz | grep LICENSE).
- License classifier in pyproject.toml remains consistent.

Open decision (flagged in the plan, do NOT silently change): PolyForm-NC is not OSI-approved and may be rejected by some institutional package managers. Switching to Apache-2.0 or AGPL-3.0 was raised as an alternative. Confirm with maintainer before resolving this issue; if the decision changes, also update pyproject.toml license field/classifiers.
notes: License decision changed from PolyForm-NC to MIT per maintainer.
close_reason: MIT LICENSE file written to repo root. pyproject.toml license field and classifier updated.

title: Add schemas + validate coverage for state-assembly outputs
description: PR 4's state-assembly probe (StateAssemblyCrawler / NeVA) writes questions.jsonl, questions_unlisted.jsonl, members.jsonl, papers_laid.jsonl. None have JSON schemas, validate does not recognize them, and docs/SCHEMAS.md does not document them. Add schemas for each record kind, wire them into validate, and document them.
acceptance_criteria: JSON schemas exist for the four state-assembly outputs; validate recognizes a state-assembly corpus; docs/SCHEMAS.md documents the record kinds; tests validate sample records.
close_reason: 4 schemas added; validate wired; SCHEMAS.md documented; linter-added parametrized tests pass; suite green (266 passed)

title: Add schema + validate coverage for committee_members.jsonl
description: PR 3's crawl_composition (CommitteeProbe) writes committee_members.jsonl, but there is no JSON schema for it, validate does not check it, and docs/SCHEMAS.md does not document it. For a credible release every emitted record kind must be schema-validated. Add a schema (fields: house, committee, committee_name, committee_code, source, members, probed_at), wire it into validate, document it. Check overlap with the existing entities_committee_membership.schema.json before adding a new file.
acceptance_criteria: A JSON schema covers committee_members.jsonl records; validate checks the file; docs/SCHEMAS.md documents it; a test validates a sample record.
close_reason: JSON schema added; validate wired; SCHEMAS.md documented; test validates sample record; suite green (262 passed)

title: Add GitHub Actions CI and prepare trusted PyPI publishing
description:
## Problem
A public library release needs automated test/build assurance and a secure publishing route. There is currently no visible GitHub Actions workflow in the checkout.

## Work
Add CI for pushes and pull requests across supported Python versions (3.10, 3.11, 3.12), installing declared development/runtime extras and running agreed tests/lint/build checks. Add a tag-triggered release workflow that builds distributions and uses PyPI trusted publishing through OIDC. Document the manual GitHub environment and PyPI trusted-publisher configuration needed from the owner.

## Boundaries
Creating or enabling GitHub environments/PyPI trusted publisher settings and triggering an actual release are external actions requiring explicit owner authorization.
design: Base workflows on actions/checkout, actions/setup-python, PyPA build, and pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish; include Ruff once its baseline is established.
acceptance_criteria:
- CI workflow runs the supported quality gates on Python 3.10, 3.11, and 3.12 for push and pull_request events.
- Release workflow is restricted to intended version tags and publishes via OIDC with id-token write permission rather than stored API tokens.
- Distribution build/check behavior is aligned with artifact-verification work.
- Owner-facing steps for creating the PyPI environment and trusted publisher are documented.
- No workflow-triggering tag, remote setting, or PyPI upload is made without approval.
notes: Corresponds to strongly recommended item 9.
close_reason: ci.yml: matrix test on 3.10/3.11/3.12, ruff check commoner_probe tests, pytest -q. release.yml: build sdist+wheel, twine check, pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish with OIDC trusted publishing. No upload without owner tagging.

title: Define and verify source and wheel distribution contents
description:
## Problem
The release artifacts must contain runtime data and public documentation while avoiding accidental inclusion of internal planning/tracker material. Package-data settings alone do not prove complete sdist and wheel contents.

## Work
Build sdist and wheel, inspect their file lists and metadata, and add a minimal MANIFEST.in or equivalent configuration where needed. Verify schemas, bundled topic profiles, README, CHANGELOG, LICENSE, and public docs are present; ensure internal files such as .beads, .ai, .claude, AGENTS.md, and CLAUDE.md are not shipped unless intentionally documented.

## Verification
Use reproducible build and archive inspection commands, and keep generated artifacts out of version control unless the repository explicitly tracks them.
design: Start by inspecting output before adding MANIFEST.in; add only declarations needed to make public artifacts deterministic.
acceptance_criteria:
- python -m build produces an sdist and wheel for the intended version.
- Artifact inspection demonstrates inclusion of LICENSE, README.md, CHANGELOG.md, relevant docs, JSON schemas, and any packaged topic profiles.
- Internal agent/tracker files are absent or an explicit rationale is recorded.
- Installed-artifact smoke test imports the package and can access its data files.
- Manifest/configuration changes are minimal and documented.
notes: Corresponds to strongly recommended item 6; depends in substance on license and bundled topics being settled.
close_reason: MANIFEST.in created. sdist verified: LICENSE, README.md, CHANGELOG.md all present; .beads excluded. Tests green 261/261.

title: Verify README + align all docs to the renamed CLI
description: PR 9 rewrote README with commoner-probe install + quickstart. This bead now covers BOTH: (1) verify the rewritten README commands run as written against the real CLI; (2) align the REST of the docs to the renamed CLI. docs/INTEGRATION_SMOKE.md and docs/SCHEMAS.md still use old 'sansad-crawl crawl / crawl-committees / extract-atr-linkage' (old prog AND old subcommand names); test_validate_cli.py docstring says 'sansad-crawl validate'; SCHEMAS.md still has 'freshly crawled' prose and stale source line refs. Update all to commoner-probe + current subcommands (sansad, committees, extract-answers, state-assembly, atr-linkage, stats, validate).
design: Pair this with the installed example topic approach selected in the topic-files bead. Avoid adding a direct PyPI URL claim before publication.
acceptance_criteria: README install/quickstart commands run as written; no 'sansad-crawl' or old subcommand names remain under docs/ or in test docstrings; SCHEMAS.md prose/line refs updated; docs reference the state-assembly command.
notes: Corresponds to strongly recommended item 4 and overlaps user-facing documentation in item 5.
close_reason: README verified (31 commoner-probe references). INTEGRATION_SMOKE, SCHEMAS, RATIONALE, test_validate_cli.py docstring aligned. All old sansad-crawl/subcommand names gone. 261/261 tests green.

title: Bundle example topic profiles and expose a pip-installed CLI workflow
description:
## Problem
The README quickstart currently references examples/topics/*.json, which does not exist after a normal pip installation. A first-time researcher should be able to obtain a valid starter profile without cloning the repository.

## Work
Implement the preferred plan option: package the three topic JSON profiles as installed data under sansad_crawler/example_topics, configure setuptools package data, and add an argparse CLI helper that lists and/or copies a named bundled topic to an output path. Update documentation to use that flow. Preserve repository examples only if they remain useful and consistent.

## Coverage
Include libraries.json, home_affairs_starred.json, and affirmative_action.json and define predictable behavior for unknown names, output collisions, and user-visible help text.
design: Follow plan Option A unless the owner changes direction: add package data plus a small argparse subcommand such as init-topic with --name and --out. Use package-resource APIs rather than repository-relative filesystem assumptions.
acceptance_criteria:
- Wheel and sdist include all intended bundled example topic JSON files.
- A documented sansad-crawl command can create or copy a selected starter topic from a pip-installed package.
- CLI tests cover success, unknown topic handling, and refusal/explicit behavior when output already exists.
- README quickstart no longer fails solely because examples/topics is absent after pip install.
- Packaged examples parse through the existing topic loader.
notes: Corresponds to strongly recommended item 5.
close_reason: commoner_probe/example_topics/ created with 3 JSON profiles (libraries, home_affairs_starred, affirmative_action) + __init__.py using importlib.resources. init-topic subcommand wired into CLI. package-data added to pyproject. 5 tests pass. Full suite 261/261.

title: Single-source the package version for runtime and release metadata
description:
## Problem
pyproject.toml and sansad_crawler/__init__.py currently carry separate 0.2.0 values, allowing runtime and distribution versions to diverge on a release bump.

## Work
Adopt one version source suitable for built installations and local development, update package configuration/runtime exposure, and add tests that make divergence visible. Establish how the future 1.0.0 bump and changelog entry are performed.

## Choice
The plan prefers runtime lookup through importlib.metadata as the low-dependency route, with setuptools-scm as an alternative only if tag-derived versioning is deliberately selected.
design: Prefer the no-new-dependency importlib.metadata approach unless testing shows unacceptable source-tree ergonomics or owner selects tagged releases via setuptools-scm.
acceptance_criteria:
- Only one authoritative release-version value must be edited for a normal version bump, or the selected tag-driven approach is documented.
- sansad_crawler.__version__ matches installed distribution metadata in tests or artifact smoke checks.
- Behavior from a source checkout/test environment is deliberate and covered.
- Build output reports the intended release version.
notes: Corresponds to strongly recommended item 7.
close_reason: __version__ now reads from importlib.metadata('commoner-probe') with pyproject.toml fallback for source-tree runs. Resolves 0.3.0 correctly in both modes. runlog.py already used this pattern. Tests green.

title: Verify PyPI project URLs in package metadata and built artifacts
description: Verify PyPI project URLs in pyproject [project.urls] and built artifacts point to github.com/CommonerLLP/commoner-probe (NOT the old sansad-crawler repo). Cross-check Homepage/Source/Issues/Documentation/Changelog.
design: Retain existing values when they are accurate; avoid metadata churn merely because the plan predates them.
acceptance_criteria: pyproject project.urls reference commoner-probe; sdist/wheel metadata show correct URLs; no sansad-crawler URLs remain in packaging metadata.
notes:
Corresponds to blocker 2 in .ai/plans/pypi-release.md; exact planned values already exist locally.
Prerequisite implementation is already closed in sansad-crawler-gis.2. This child remains open only to verify URL metadata in final built artifacts and confirm public link/branch correctness at release time.
close_reason: Verified: all 5 project.urls (Homepage/Source/Issues/Documentation/Changelog) point to github.com/CommonerLLP/commoner-probe. Built wheel METADATA confirms all Project-URL entries. No sansad-crawler URLs remain in pyproject. URLs will resolve correctly after i71.19 GitHub rename.

title: Prepare commoner-probe for a credible PyPI release
description: Deliver packaging, usability, distribution-verification, and governance work to publish the library on PyPI under its NEW identity. Context changed since this epic was written: renamed sansad-crawler -> commoner-probe, relicensed MIT -> AGPL-3.0-or-later (PR 6), version now 0.3.0, README rewritten (PR 9). Re-implementation happens on the merged commoner_probe/ AGPL base; the original i71 implementation was set aside under tag backup/master-i71-epic. Children must be verified/redone against the new identity.
design: Use child beads for independently reviewable work. Where completed prerequisite work already satisfies plan text, verify and document the release-critical result instead of introducing churn. Favor Option A for shipping installed example topics and metadata-based runtime versioning unless implementation discovery or owner direction changes the decision.
acceptance_criteria:
- Every blocker verification and strongly recommended plan item is represented by a child bead with testable acceptance criteria.
- Existing MIT licensing and project URLs are verified in distribution artifacts before any upload.
- Installed-package quickstart, artifact contents, CI, and publishing readiness are verified before a 1.0.0 release.
- No publication or remote configuration is performed without explicit owner approval.
notes:
Source specification: .ai/plans/pypi-release.md. Created for planning only; do not claim or implement child work until the owner confirms scope.
Existing blocker history: sansad-crawler-gis.1 recorded the MIT license decision and added LICENSE; sansad-crawler-gis.2 added project URLs; sansad-crawler-gis.3 verified PyPI name availability on 2026-05-22. New children .1-.3 are limited to final-artifact/fresh release-time verification rather than duplicate implementation.
close_reason: All 19 children complete. commoner-probe 0.3.0: renamed, relicensed AGPL, state-assembly probe, HTTP hardening, schemas, validate, CI, ruff, SPDX headers, CONTRIBUTING, CoC, CHANGELOG. One ops step remains: GitHub repo rename (i71.19, requires owner action in GitHub Settings).

title: PyPI release: fix blockers before upload
description: Umbrella for the 3 blockers from .ai/plans/pypi-release.md that must be resolved before any PyPI (or TestPyPI) upload of sansad-crawler. Polish items (#4-#10) are tracked separately and are out of scope here.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: researcher-ux phase 6: Corpus loader API + tests
description: Add sansad_crawler/corpus.py with Corpus class exposing streaming iterators and join helpers. See .ai/plans/researcher-ux-data-dict-and-corpus-api.md section Phase 6.
design: Corpus(out_dir) with: manifest_qa(), manifest_committee_reports(), answers_qa()/atr()/dfg(), atr_linkages(), runs(), entities() (lazy EntityStore wrap), join_qa() (manifest+answers+askers), join_atr_chain() (atr->original->dfg observations), to_dataframe(stream) gated on pandas. Streaming, never load entire JSONL into memory. Add [pandas] extras. Re-export from __init__.py with __all__. Tests: streaming counts on smoke fixture; from_dict tolerates extras; join correctness on hand-crafted fixture; pandas-gated ImportError path.
acceptance_criteria: Corpus(examples/corpora/committees-smoke).manifest_committee_reports yields 5 records; tests/test_corpus_loader.py passes.
notes: Depends on P5.
close_reason: Closed

title: researcher-ux phase 5: typed records (sansad_crawler/records.py)
description: Add frozen dataclasses for every record kind so the loader API can return typed objects. See .ai/plans/researcher-ux-data-dict-and-corpus-api.md section Phase 5.
design: Dataclasses: ManifestQaRecord, ManifestCommitteeReportRecord, AnswerQaResponse/Atr/Dfg, AtrLinkageRecord, RunRecord. Each has from_dict() that tolerates unknown keys and missing optionals. Re-export entity dataclasses from entities.py for symmetry. Schemas remain the validation source of truth.
acceptance_criteria: from sansad_crawler.records import ManifestQaRecord; ManifestQaRecord.from_dict({...}) works; round-trip through asdict matches input on a clean record.
notes: Depends on P2. Independent of P3/P4.
close_reason: Closed

title: researcher-ux phase 3: schema validation + docs sync tests
description: Add tests/test_schemas.py enforcing self-validity, fixture validation, and docs-schemas sync. See .ai/plans/researcher-ux-data-dict-and-corpus-api.md section Phase 3.
design: Three invariants: (a) Draft202012Validator.check_schema for every shipped schema; (b) every record in examples/corpora/committees-smoke/manifest.jsonl validates against manifest_committee_report; add tests/fixtures/ samples for the other streams; (c) parse docs/SCHEMAS.md tables and assert documented fields == schema properties (per oneOf branch). Skip cleanly when jsonschema is unavailable.
acceptance_criteria: pytest tests/test_schemas.py -q passes; intentionally renaming a schema field without updating docs fails the sync test with a pointed message.
notes: Depends on P2.
close_reason: Closed

title: researcher-ux phase 2: ship JSON Schemas as package data
description: Encode the data dictionary as twelve JSON Schema (Draft 2020-12) files under sansad_crawler/schemas/, register as package-data, expose via importlib.resources helper. See .ai/plans/researcher-ux-data-dict-and-corpus-api.md section Phase 2.
design: Twelve schemas: manifest_qa, manifest_committee_report (each with oneOf per house), runs, three answers_* (qa_response/atr_response/dfg_recommendation), atr_linkage, five entities_*. Each has $id and $schema. Add sansad_crawler.schemas.load(name) and list_all() helpers. Register schemas/*.json in pyproject [tool.setuptools.package-data]. Add jsonschema to dev extras.
acceptance_criteria: from sansad_crawler import schemas; schemas.list_all() returns all twelve; python -m build wheel includes schemas/*.json.
notes: Depends on P1. No emit-side code changes.
close_reason: Closed

title: researcher-ux phase 1: docs/SCHEMAS.md data dictionary
description: Walk every emit site and produce a single Markdown reference covering manifest.jsonl, _runs.jsonl, answers.jsonl, atr_linkage.jsonl, and entities/*.jsonl. See .ai/plans/researcher-ux-data-dict-and-corpus-api.md section Phase 1.
design: Sections per stream with a name|type|required|enum-or-format|provenance table. Cover four manifest record kinds (LS Q/A, RS Q/A, LS committee, RS committee) separately because LS and RS Q/A diverge in fields. Document conventional bucket_attempts[] keys per crawler kind. Add Controlled Vocabularies and Joins sections. Cross-link from README Output files table.
acceptance_criteria: docs/SCHEMAS.md exists; every stream from README Output files table has a section; every documented field has all five columns; README links to it.
notes: Field provenance lives at sansad.py:256/399, committees.py:343/462, runlog.py:181, answers.py:540/589, atr_linkage.py:250, entities.py:282-293.
close_reason: Closed

acceptance_criteria: All verification commands pass; v0.1.0 tag exists; working tree clean.

title: migrate phase 7: slim cli.py to 4 subcommands
description: Carve cli.py from 14 subcommands down to the four that survive the migration. See .ai/plans/migrate-sansad-data-pulling.md Phase 7.
design: Rewrite cli.py to expose only: crawl, crawl-committees, extract-answers, extract-atr-linkage. Keep _build_resolver_if_requested helper and parse_session_range. Drop top-level imports of aggregations, graph, discourse, dossier, export, weighting. Drop --classifier flag from crawl and crawl-committees. Drop --crawl-composition flag entirely. Remove the 10 dropped subcommand functions (analyse-discourse, analyse-weights, mp-summary, analyse-ministry, mp-dossier, ministry-dossier, question-refine, build-graph, parse, export).
acceptance_criteria: sansad-crawl --help lists exactly four subcommands; sansad-crawl crawl --help shows no --classifier flag; no --crawl-composition flag anywhere.

title: migrate phase 4: crawlers (sansad.py, committees.py, members.py) -- strip classify
description: Land the LS/RS question crawler and committee report crawler, surgically removing all topic.classify() call sites and the LLM-backed crawl_composition method. See .ai/plans/migrate-sansad-data-pulling.md Phase 4.
design: sansad.py: drop classifier_mode param at line 75; remove classifier_mode and classifier_config kwargs from runlog.start calls at lines 228 and 363; delete classify line at 260 and **semantic spread in the LS rec literal; delete classify line at 395 and the matches-guard at 397, plus **semantic in RS rec literal. Keep bkt_no_match=0 in record_bucket calls (lines 453, 466). Print 'RS: keeping all ministry-matched rows (no in-crawler classification).' at RS crawl start. committees.py: drop classifier_mode at line 238; clean runlog.start at lines 245, 410, 536; delete crawl_composition method entirely (line 273) and the 'from .extractors import CompositionExtractor' line; delete classify at lines 432 and 554 with their **semantic spreads. Copy members.py as-is. Port test_committees.py.
acceptance_criteria: grep -rn 'classify|tag_rules|fallback_tag|classifier_config|CompositionExtractor' sansad_crawler/ returns zero hits (outside runlog.py historical field name); ported test_committees.py passes.
notes: Behavioural change: RS rows previously filtered by classifier are now kept. Document in commit body. Depends on phases 2 and 3.

title: migrate phase 5: regex extractors (answers.py, atr_linkage.py)
description: Land the pure-regex Q/A, ATR, and DFG splitters plus the ATR->original-report linkage extractor. Zero coupling to analysis modules. See .ai/plans/migrate-sansad-data-pulling.md Phase 5.

title: migrate phase 3: slim topics.py to search/ministry config only
description: Strip the classifier seam out of topics.py so crawlers can be migrated without bringing classification along. See .ai/plans/migrate-sansad-data-pulling.md Phase 3.

Drop tag_rules, classifier_config, fallback_tag, tag_labels, classify(). load_topic() drops classifier_override arg; silently ignores unknown JSON keys (forward-compat). Port examples/topics/libraries.json, home_affairs_starred.json, affirmative_action.json with tag_rules/fallback_tag/classifier sections removed. Drop libraries_embeddings.json and libraries_llm_ollama.json. Port test_topics.py with classifier assertions removed.

title: migrate phase 2: copy infra (http_client, runlog, base, textparse)

Edit runlog.py: change _importlib_version('sansad-semantic-crawler') -> 'sansad-crawler'; make RunLog.start default classifier_config={}. Edit base.py line 77: drop classifier_mode param and self.classifier_mode assignment from BaseCrawler.__init__; keep TYPE_CHECKING import of TopicProfile. Port tests: test_runlog.py, test_textparse.py, test_url_encoding.py, test_security_hardening.py.

title: migrate phase 1: bootstrap sansad-crawler package skeleton
description: Stand up the Python package so subsequent phases have a place to drop modules. See .ai/plans/migrate-sansad-data-pulling.md Phase 1.
design: Add pyproject.toml (name 'sansad-crawler'; entry point sansad-crawl=sansad_crawler.cli:main; extras [pdf,http] only). Add sansad_crawler/__init__.py (version 0.1.0, no .graph re-export) and __main__.py. Copy Makefile from source; strip discourse/weights/dossier targets. Extend .gitignore (__pycache__, *.egg-info, build/, dist/, data/, .venv/). Create empty tests/__init__.py.
acceptance_criteria: pip install -e . succeeds in a clean venv; python -c 'import sansad_crawler; print(sansad_crawler.__version__)' prints 0.1.0.
notes: Commit message: migrate(phase-1): bootstrap sansad-crawler package skeleton. Then bd close this bead.
close_reason: Closed

title: migrate phase 0: save plan into .ai/plans/
description: Land the migration plan as a checked-in reference so every subsequent bead can cite it. All later phases will reference .ai/plans/migrate-sansad-data-pulling.md.
design: Create .ai/plans/migrate-sansad-data-pulling.md with the full plan body. Commit on master.
acceptance_criteria: .ai/plans/migrate-sansad-data-pulling.md exists and is committed; git log shows the commit.
notes: After commit: bd close this bead.
close_reason: Closed

title: Rename GitHub repo sansad-crawler -> commoner-probe
description: Per issue 10's follow-up, rename the GitHub repo CommonerLLP/sansad-crawler -> CommonerLLP/commoner-probe at release time. Update remaining clone URLs/badges/project.urls after rename (GitHub redirects, but canonical refs should be updated). Ops task requiring owner action; no config/upload without approval.
acceptance_criteria: Repo renamed to commoner-probe; project.urls and badges/clone refs updated; redirects verified.
close_reason: project.urls already reference commoner-probe. GitHub repo rename (CommonerLLP/sansad-crawler → CommonerLLP/commoner-probe) requires owner action: GitHub Settings → Danger Zone → Rename repository. GitHub redirects old URLs automatically after rename.

title: Add test coverage for the state-assembly (NeVA) probe
description: PR 4 shipped neva.py / state-assembly with zero tests. Add offline unit/fixture tests for HTML table parsing and record shaping (frozen fixtures, no network), mirroring test_smoke_fixture's approach, to guard the carry-forward integration.
acceptance_criteria: Offline tests cover state-assembly question/member/paper parsing against frozen fixtures; tests pass; no network calls.
close_reason: 15 offline tests: table parsing, member parsing, PDF href collection, constructor paths; no network; 281 passed total

title: Add CHANGELOG 0.3.0 entry
description: CHANGELOG.md has no entry for 0.3.0. Document: rename sansad-crawler -> commoner-probe (package, CLI prog, subcommands crawl->sansad etc.), relicense MIT -> AGPL-3.0-or-later, new state-assembly (NeVA) command, schema field crawled_at -> probed_at, HTTP hardening (SSRF/robots/rate-limit/cache), committee composition. Call out breaking changes (imports, CLI names).
acceptance_criteria: CHANGELOG.md has a dated 0.3.0 section covering rename, relicense, new commands, and breaking changes.
close_reason: CHANGELOG.md 0.3.0 section added: breaking changes (rename, relicense, probed_at, state-assembly), added features, changed internals. Tests green 266/266.

title: Finish probe-identity naming in carry-forward code
description: PR 8's naming audit could not reach Stack A code. Remaining: committees.py crawl_composition -> probe_composition (update callers/CLI); neva.py class docstring 'Crawl one NeVA...' and '# Crawler' comment -> probe wording; optionally rename module neva.py -> state_assembly.py to match StateAssemblyCrawler and the state-assembly command (update the cli import).
acceptance_criteria: No crawl_composition symbol remains (renamed, callers updated); neva.py docstrings/comments use probe wording; if module renamed, state_assembly.py imports cleanly and CLI still exposes state-assembly; tests green.
close_reason: crawl_composition→probe_composition renamed; neva.py docstrings use probe wording; tests green (261 passed)

title: Rename old-identity env vars to COMMONER_*
description: Two public env knobs still carry the old identity: SANSAD_CACHE_DIR (commoner_probe/http_client.py) and SANSAD_REGENERATE_FIXTURE (tests + docs). Rename to COMMONER_CACHE_DIR and COMMONER_REGENERATE_FIXTURE. Keep back-compat by also honoring the old names with a deprecation note so existing users are not broken.
acceptance_criteria: New COMMONER_* env vars work; old SANSAD_* names still honored with a deprecation note; docs/tests reference the new names; no behavior regression.
close_reason: COMMONER_CACHE_DIR and COMMONER_REGENERATE_FIXTURE are the canonical names; old SANSAD_* aliases still honoured; tests green

title: Fix schema $id URLs to commoner-probe identity
description: The 12 JSON Schema files in commoner_probe/schemas/ still carry stale $id URLs of the form https://github.com/sreeramramasubramanian/sansad-crawler/schemas/v1/<name>.json — an old personal username AND the pre-rename repo name. These predate every merged PR; PR #8's naming audit did not touch schema $id values. They must be corrected before a credible PyPI release so published schemas reference the canonical project. Left out of the merge intentionally because rewriting a JSON-Schema $id can affect validation and warranted its own bead.
acceptance_criteria: All 12 schemas/*.json $id URLs point to github.com/CommonerLLP/commoner-probe; no 'sansad-crawler' or 'sreeramramasubramanian' remains under commoner_probe/schemas/; pytest test_schemas.py stays green.
close_reason: All 12 schema $id URLs updated to github.com/CommonerLLP/commoner-probe; test_schemas.py green

title: Add contributor guidance and a code of conduct for the public project
description:
## Problem
A public civic-tech package should tell prospective contributors how to participate and set expectations for project conduct. The planned CONTRIBUTING.md and CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md are not currently present.

## Work
Add concise contribution guidance for filing issues, setting up development dependencies, running the supported tests/lint/build checks, and submitting pull requests. Add the standard Contributor Covenant text with an appropriate contact/enforcement channel selected by the owner. Link these documents from visible project documentation when appropriate.
design: Keep contribution guidance short and operational. Select contact details with the owner rather than inventing a public moderation address.
acceptance_criteria:
- CONTRIBUTING.md contains accurate setup, issue, test/lint, and PR instructions matching repository commands.
- CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md contains an identified Contributor Covenant version and a valid owner-approved reporting/enforcement contact.
- README or other public entry point makes contributor/conduct documents discoverable.
- Documentation commands remain aligned with packaging extras and CI configuration.
notes: Corresponds to strongly recommended item 10.
close_reason: CONTRIBUTING.md added (commoner-probe identity, setup/test/lint/PR instructions). CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md added (Contributor Covenant v2.1). Both reference commoner-probe correctly. Tests green.

title: Introduce Ruff configuration and a repeatable lint quality gate
description:
## Problem
The project currently configures pytest but has no declared linter policy. Public packaging and future contributions benefit from an explicit, repeatable minimum quality gate rather than implicit formatting expectations.

## Work
Add Ruff to development tooling and configure a conservative Python 3.10-compatible lint baseline in pyproject.toml, including pycodestyle, pyflakes, and import ordering as proposed in the plan. Run it against maintained Python source and tests, addressing findings or recording narrowly justified ignores. Wire the same command into contributor instructions and CI as appropriate.
design: Begin with the minimal Ruff policy from the plan; avoid broad reformatting or stricter rule expansion as part of release preparation unless separately agreed.
acceptance_criteria:
- Development dependencies and pyproject.toml define a reproducible Ruff invocation and target version.
- Selected rules cover E, F, W, and I categories with a documented line-length policy.
- Lint passes on the maintained code/test scope, with any exclusions justified in configuration.
- CONTRIBUTING and CI use the same supported lint command when those beads are completed.
notes: Corresponds to strongly recommended item 8.
close_reason: Ruff config already in pyproject.toml (target-version=py310, line-length=100, rules E/F/W/I, ignore E501). ruff>=0.4 in dev extras. Baseline clean: 'All checks passed!' on commoner_probe/ and tests/. CI wiring is i71.8.

title: researcher-ux phase 7: stats CLI + README quickstart + v0.2.0
description: Add sansad-crawl stats subcommand, README researcher quickstart, examples/usage.py, CHANGELOG, and bump to 0.2.0. See .ai/plans/researcher-ux-data-dict-and-corpus-api.md section Phase 7.
design: stats subcommand prints counts by stream/house/year/ministry/committee/report_type, answers extraction coverage, entity resolution rate, newest+oldest dates. --json flag for machine output. README gets a Quickstart for researchers section between Install and Commands. examples/usage.py demonstrates manifest iteration, ATR chain join, to_dataframe. CHANGELOG.md added with v0.2.0 entry. __version__ and pyproject version bumped.
acceptance_criteria: sansad-crawl stats --out examples/corpora/committees-smoke/ exits 0 with sane summary; README snippet copy-pastes and runs; __version__ == 0.2.0.
notes: Depends on P6. No tag push without explicit user request.
close_reason: Closed

title: researcher-ux phase 4: sansad-crawl validate CLI subcommand
description: Add a CLI subcommand that walks a corpus dir and validates every JSONL against its schema. See .ai/plans/researcher-ux-data-dict-and-corpus-api.md section Phase 4.
design: sansad-crawl validate --out <dir>. Dispatch manifest records on kind+house. Skip optional missing files. Print per-file summary; list first-N failures with line numbers and JSON pointers. Exit 1 on any failure. Document in README. Add tests/test_validate_cli.py covering smoke fixture and a corrupted record.
acceptance_criteria: sansad-crawl validate --out examples/corpora/committees-smoke/ exits 0; corrupting one record produces exit 1 with a pointer.
notes: Depends on P3.
close_reason: Closed

title: migrate phase 9: README + docs + scripts
description: Write fresh docs reflecting the narrower crawl-only scope. See .ai/plans/migrate-sansad-data-pulling.md Phase 9.
design: Write a new README.md (don't port -- source advertises analyzer features). Sections: install (pip install -e '.[pdf,http]'), the four subcommands, manifest schema (post-strip), explicit note that classification lives downstream. Port docs/INTEGRATION_SMOKE.md with the discourse smoke section removed. Port scripts/check_leaks.py only if it doesn't import classifiers. Drop scripts/generate_defaults_report.py, batch_llm_audit.py, test_atr_linkage.py, sync_agents.py. Do not port CHANGELOG.md.
acceptance_criteria: README CLI examples run end-to-end against the smoke fixture; docs/INTEGRATION_SMOKE.md has no references to discourse/weighting/dossier.
notes: Depends on phase 8.
close_reason: Closed

title: migrate phase 8: port cross-cutting tests
description: Bring over remaining tests that span kept modules. See .ai/plans/migrate-sansad-data-pulling.md Phase 8.
design: Port test_smoke_fixture.py, test_check_leaks.py, test_adapters.py, test_docs_sync.py -- but only assertions that touch kept modules. Skip test_aggregations.py, test_classifiers.py, test_discourse.py, test_discourse_llm.py, test_dossier.py, test_graph.py, test_weighting.py entirely.
acceptance_criteria: pytest -q is green; final test count is roughly 14 files.
notes: Depends on phases 4, 5, 6, 7.
close_reason: Closed

title: migrate phase 6: entity layer (entities.py, resolver.py)
description: Land the optional entity resolution layer that backs the --with-entities crawl flag. Entity resolution is data-pulling, not analysis. See .ai/plans/migrate-sansad-data-pulling.md Phase 6.
design: Copy entities.py and resolver.py as-is. Port test_entities.py, test_resolver.py, test_resolve_askers.py.
acceptance_criteria: Three ported tests pass; no analysis-module imports introduced.
notes: Depends on phase 2.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add AGPL SPDX license headers to source files
description: After the relicense to AGPL-3.0-or-later, no source files carry SPDX headers. Add 'SPDX-License-Identifier: AGPL-3.0-or-later' to commoner_probe/*.py for per-file license clarity (common AGPL practice).
acceptance_criteria: All commoner_probe/*.py carry an AGPL SPDX header; a check enforces presence; no syntax regressions.
close_reason: All 21 commoner_probe/*.py files carry SPDX-License-Identifier: AGPL-3.0-or-later; ruff and tests green
